scenario: Global event vs prop callback for child-to-parent communication.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: Context with callback for deep cases.

decision_framework: debate-301: judge the state management architecture debate in this scenario: Global event vs prop callback for child-to-parent communication.. Separate claims, evidence, counterexamples, operational consequences, migration cost, and the failure mode each side handles poorly.
what_juniors_miss: Juniors drill 5+ levels. Senior insight: at depth, context wins.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: For trivial defaults, inline. For complex, component.

weaknesses: Weakness: this option still needs production validation for Slot pattern with default content., especially failure modes and team familiarity.

decision_framework: debate-302: judge the component composition patterns debate in this scenario: Slot pattern with default content.. Separate claims, evidence, counterexamples, operational consequences, migration cost, and the failure mode each side handles poorly.
what_juniors_miss: Juniors no defaults. Senior insight: defaults are part of API design.

sam_initial_position: Both. Inline + summary at top for screen readers.

debate.speaker: sam
debate.argument: Summary helps screen readers navigate to errors.

decision_framework: debate-303: judge the form library choices debate in this scenario: Form errors: inline vs summary.. Separate claims, evidence, counterexamples, operational consequences, migration cost, and the failure mode each side handles poorly.
what_juniors_miss: Juniors inline only. Senior insight: SR users benefit from error summary.

context: Polling continues even on hidden tab.
alex_initial_position: Pause when hidden. Page Visibility API.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: Hidden tab doesn't need updates. Pause saves resources.

context_dependent_answer: Pause when hidden, refetch on visible.
decision_framework: debate-304: judge the data fetching strategy debate in this scenario: Polling backoff when tab hidden.. Separate claims, evidence, counterexamples, operational consequences, migration cost, and the failure mode each side handles poorly.
what_juniors_miss: Juniors poll forever. Senior insight: hidden tabs waste resources.

scenario: Modal overlay: position fixed vs portal.
alex_initial_position: Portal to document.body. Escapes stacking contexts.

debate.speaker: sam
debate.argument: Fixed at app root achieves same.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: Portal at body level is safer (escapes overflow:hidden).

decision_framework: debate-305: judge the styling approach wars debate in this scenario: Modal overlay: position fixed vs portal.. Separate claims, evidence, counterexamples, operational consequences, migration cost, and the failure mode each side handles poorly.
what_juniors_miss: Juniors don't portal. Senior insight: stacking contexts trap fixed elements unexpectedly.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: Skeleton communicates layout. Better perceived perf.

decision_framework: debate-306: judge the animation library decisions debate in this scenario: Page load skeleton vs spinner.. Separate claims, evidence, counterexamples, operational consequences, migration cost, and the failure mode each side handles poorly.
what_juniors_miss: Juniors spinner everything. Senior insight: skeleton communicates structure.

scenario: Snapshot tests with inline vs file.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: Inline review with test. Don't need separate file.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: For small snapshots: inline. Large: file.

decision_framework: debate-307: judge the testing philosophy debate in this scenario: Snapshot tests with inline vs file.. Separate claims, evidence, counterexamples, operational consequences, migration cost, and the failure mode each side handles poorly.
what_juniors_miss: Juniors snapshot everything. Senior insight: snapshot quality matters.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: Overloads give precise return type per input.

what_juniors_miss: Juniors use 'any' for polymorphic returns. Senior insight: overloads enable precise types.

scenario: Tab visibility throttling: pause vs continue.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: Hidden tab doesn't need animation. Pause.

debate.speaker: sam
debate.argument: rAF already throttles in hidden tabs.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: rAF throttles to ~1fps. Better to fully pause for explicit control.

decision_framework: debate-309: judge the performance vs dx tradeoffs debate in this scenario: Tab visibility throttling: pause vs continue.. Separate claims, evidence, counterexamples, operational consequences, migration cost, and the failure mode each side handles poorly.
what_juniors_miss: Juniors rAF runs always. Senior insight: hidden tab work wastes battery.

scenario: Deno vs Node for tooling scripts.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: Deno runs TS natively. No tsx/ts-node setup.

debate.speaker: sam
debate.argument: Node has more compat with npm packages.

debate.speaker: sam
debate.argument: For scripts: Deno fine. App runtime: Node still default.

context_dependent_answer: Scripts: Deno or Bun. App: Node.
decision_framework: debate-310: judge the build tool choices debate in this scenario: Deno vs Node for tooling scripts.. Separate claims, evidence, counterexamples, operational consequences, migration cost, and the failure mode each side handles poorly.
what_juniors_miss: Juniors npm for everything. Senior insight: Deno simplifies TS scripts.

scenario: Component lib: source vs compiled distribution.

debate.speaker: sam
debate.argument: Source allows consumer's bundler to tree-shake better.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: Compiled with proper sideEffects: false trees-shakes too.

decision_framework: debate-311: judge the component library strategy debate in this scenario: Component lib: source vs compiled distribution.. Separate claims, evidence, counterexamples, operational consequences, migration cost, and the failure mode each side handles poorly.
what_juniors_miss: Juniors ship source. Senior insight: compiled with proper flags is best of both.

scenario: User session expiry: silent refresh vs explicit logout.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: Silent refresh keeps user logged in.

debate.speaker: sam
debate.argument: Refresh tokens add complexity, security concerns.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: With httpOnly cookies, refresh is safe.

decision_framework: debate-312: judge the error boundary strategy debate in this scenario: User session expiry: silent refresh vs explicit logout.. Separate claims, evidence, counterexamples, operational consequences, migration cost, and the failure mode each side handles poorly.
what_juniors_miss: Juniors logout on 401. Senior insight: silent refresh improves UX.

alex_initial_position: Use modulepreload for entry + critical deps.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: modulepreload tells browser to fetch + parse + compile.

context_dependent_answer: Verify Vite inserts. Manual if needed.

code_examples.code:
<head>
  <!-- Vite inserts these automatically for entry + chunks -->
  <link rel="modulepreload" href="/assets/index-hash.js" />
  <link rel="modulepreload" href="/assets/vendor-hash.js" />
  <script type="module" src="/assets/index-hash.js"></script>
</head>

decision_framework: debate-313: judge the code splitting philosophy debate in this scenario: Asset preloading: <link rel='modulepreload'>.. Separate claims, evidence, counterexamples, operational consequences, migration cost, and the failure mode each side handles poorly.
what_juniors_miss: Juniors don't know modulepreload. Senior insight: ES modules benefit from preload.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: <select> works perfectly for screen readers, mobile.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: CSS appearance: base-select is coming. For now, custom or native trade-off.

debate.speaker: sam
debate.argument: For simple: native. Rich: custom with a11y primitive.

code_examples.code:
<select className="w-full px-3 py-2 rounded-md border bg-surface focus:ring-2 focus:ring-primary">
  <option value="">Choose...</option>
  <option value="a">Option A</option>
  <option value="b">Option B</option>
</select>

/* CSS can style select reasonably now */

decision_framework: debate-314: judge the accessibility tradeoffs debate in this scenario: Custom dropdown vs <select>.. Separate claims, evidence, counterexamples, operational consequences, migration cost, and the failure mode each side handles poorly.
what_juniors_miss: Juniors custom everything. Senior insight: native widgets have native a11y.

alex_initial_position: HTTP + React Query + LocalStorage. Each layer separate.
sam_initial_position: React Query handles all client cache.

debate.speaker: sam
debate.argument: React Query persister can use LocalStorage. Single API.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: HTTP cache is still distinct layer (browser-managed).

context_dependent_answer: HTTP + React Query (+ persister). Don't reinvent.
decision_framework: debate-315: judge the frontend architecture patterns debate in this scenario: Frontend caching strategies layered.. Separate claims, evidence, counterexamples, operational consequences, migration cost, and the failure mode each side handles poorly.
what_juniors_miss: Juniors no cache. Senior insight: layered cache is the pattern.

sam_initial_position: Lift to context if used in many places.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: Lift to direct parent. Minimum scope.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: Lift to lowest common. Refactor to context when proliferation.

decision_framework: debate-316: judge the state management architecture debate in this scenario: Local state colocation vs lifting.. Separate claims, evidence, counterexamples, operational consequences, migration cost, and the failure mode each side handles poorly.
what_juniors_miss: Juniors duplicate state. Senior insight: state lifting prevents sync bugs.

scenario: Form state in URL vs in form library.

debate.argument: URL changes on every keystroke = bad UX.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: Object.keys returns string[]. Cast to keyof for type safety.

debate.speaker: sam
debate.argument: Cast can be wrong (object has extra keys at runtime).

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: For known shape, cast is safe.

context_dependent_answer: Cast for closed types. Don't for open.

what_juniors_miss: Juniors don't type iteration. Senior insight: explicit cast for closed types is safer than untyped.
